basics.name: Junteng Liu
basics.summary: First-year PhD candidate at HKUST NLP Group. Research focuses on natural language processing and machine learning. Research interests include LLM Reasoning and Reinforcement Learning, Hallucination in Vision-Language Models, and LLM truthfulness and Interpretability.

work.name: Research Intern
work.summary: Researching LLM Reasoning and Reinforcement Learning at MINIMAX, an AI/technology company.

work.name: Research Intern
work.summary: Researching VLM hallucination mitigation at Tencent WXG under the supervision of Zifei Shan.

work.name: Research Intern
work.summary: Researching C-Eval at Shanghai AI Lab under the supervision of Prof. Yu Cheng.

education.institution: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

publications.name: SynLogic: Synthesizing Verifiable Reasoning Data at Scale for Learning Logical Reasoning and Beyond
publications.summary: This paper presents SynLogic, a novel framework for synthesizing verifiable reasoning data at scale for learning logical reasoning. We demonstrate that our approach can generate high-quality reasoning examples that improve model performance on logical reasoning benchmarks.

publications.name: On the Perception Bottleneck of VLMs for Chart Understanding
publications.summary: We investigate the perception bottleneck in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) for chart understanding tasks. Our analysis reveals key limitations and provides insights for improving VLM performance on chart-related tasks.

publications.name: On the Universal Truthfulness Hyperplane Inside LLMs
publications.summary: We discover a universal truthfulness hyperplane inside large language models that can be used to detect and mitigate hallucinations. Our approach provides a novel perspective on understanding and improving LLM truthfulness.

publications.name: In-Context Sharpness as Alerts: An Inner Representation Perspective for Hallucination Mitigation
publications.summary: We propose a novel perspective for hallucination mitigation by analyzing in-context sharpness as alerts from an inner representation viewpoint. Our method demonstrates improved hallucination detection and reduction.

publications.name: C-Eval: A Multi-Level Multi-Discipline Chinese Evaluation Suite for Foundation Models
publications.summary: C-Eval is a comprehensive Chinese evaluation suite for foundation models covering multiple disciplines and levels. We introduce a novel evaluation framework that provides rigorous assessment of Chinese language understanding and reasoning capabilities.

publications.name: Composing Parameter-Efficient Modules with Arithmetic Operations
publications.summary: We present a novel approach for composing parameter-efficient modules using arithmetic operations, enabling flexible and efficient model adaptation with minimal computational overhead.
